title: GAMSU Foundation & Dolly Children Foundation Expand Summer 9.0 Program Across Nigeria, Empowering Children in Multiple States - FinancialEDGE
description: The Dolly Children Foundation (DCF) has successfully concluded its four-week free Summer 9.0 program, a holiday initiative designed to provide academic support, digital skills, and creative empowerment for children. This year, the program expanded its reach beyond community-based centers to impact children across Ogun, Lagos, Oyo, and Abuja. The program, under the leadership of Founder and Convener Adedolapo
html:
The Dolly Children Foundation (DCF) has successfully concluded its four-week free Summer 9.0 program, a holiday initiative designed to provide academic support, digital skills, and creative empowerment for children. This year, the program expanded its reach beyond community-based centers to impact children across Ogun, Lagos, Oyo, and Abuja.

The program, under the leadership of Founder and Convener Adedolapo Osuntuyi, provided structured learning opportunities in literacy, numeracy, arts, and technology. A key highlight was the digital creativity track, where children advanced from basic computer use to developing websites with AI and designing storybooks with Canva.

The Gamaliel and Susan Onosode Foundation (GAMSU Foundation) played a pivotal role in ensuring the success of this year’s program. GAMSU funded teachers’ salaries and donated back-to-school materials, guaranteeing that the initiative remained free for all participants while also encouraging the teachers who committed their expertise.

Speaking at the closing ceremony, Adedolapo Osuntuyi, Founder of Dolly Children Foundation, stated that Summer 9.0 has once again demonstrated that when children are given the right platform, they thrive. We are immensely grateful to the Gamaliel and Susan Onosode Foundation for their unwavering support, which has allowed us to expand our footprint across Ogun, Lagos, Oyo, and Abuja. Our vision is to take this initiative even further, ensuring that more children in underserved communities benefit from this life-changing program.”

In her remarks, Mrs. Toyin Olanrewaju, Executive Director of Gamaliel and Susan Onosode Foundation, emphasized that organisation believes that access to quality education and equal opportunities are fundamental to national development. Partnering with the Dolly Children Foundation aligns perfectly with our mission to empower the next generation. By ensuring that no child is left behind due to limited resources, we are building the foundations for a brighter and more inclusive future.”

Commenting on the program’s community impact, Busayo Akomolafe, Chairman of the Makogi Community, noted that instead of roaming the streets during the holiday, these children were engaged in activities that can shape their future. The program is transformative, and I encourage parents to seize the opportunity in future editions.”

Similarly, Olanitori Abolade, Women Leader of Magboro, remarked that the creativity and confidence displayed by the children were truly inspiring. This program is impactful, and we commend the organizers’ dedication. It is a blessing not just to the children, but also to the entire community.”

With strategic backing from the Gamaliel and Susan Onosode Foundation, DCF’s Summer 9.0 has reaffirmed its mission to bridge the educational and digital divide for underserved children. Preparations are already underway for Summer 10.0, with plans to extend the program’s reach even further across Nigeria.
